name: Clock
description:
This algorithm produces and/or receives analogue clock pulses and/or MIDI clock. It is designed to synchronise the module with others, or with other devices that it communicates with via MIDI, or simply to be a source of clock pulses for other algorithms within the module.

It can generate its own BPM-based clock, or sync to external clock pulses, or sync to MIDI.

It can output clock pulses, or send MIDI clock, or both.

If syncing to external clock, the algorithm expects a 24ppqn DINsync style clock. If you only have a slower clock available, use the Clock multiplier algorithm to get the clock up to the right speed before this algorithm sees it.
gui_description: The display shows the current tempo, and the transport location in bars, beats, and sixteenths. It also shows the clock source, and whether the transport is running or stopped. The GUI can also show information about the swing settings.

specifications.name: Outputs
specifications.description: The number of clock outputs to generate.

parameters.name: Source
parameters.description: The clock source.

parameters.name: Tempo
parameters.description: The internal clock tempo.

parameters.name: Run
parameters.description: Starts and stops the internal clock.

parameters.name: Time sig numerator
parameters.description: The time signature numerator.

parameters.name: Time sig denominator
parameters.description: The time signature denominator: one of 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16.

parameters.name: Output to breakout
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the breakout.

parameters.name: Output to Select Bus
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the Select Bus.

parameters.name: Output to USB
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to USB.

parameters.name: Output to internal
parameters.description: Enables internal MIDI output – that is, MIDI is sent to the other algorithms.

parameters.name: Swing type
parameters.description: The type of swing to apply.

parameters.name: Swing
parameters.description: The amount of swing to apply.

parameters.name: 16th note 1
parameters.description: The position of the 1st 16th note, when using tuplet swing.

parameters.name: 16th note 2
parameters.description: The position of the 2nd 16th note, when using tuplet swing.

parameters.name: 16th note 3
parameters.description: The position of the 3rd 16th note, when using tuplet swing.

parameters.name: Enable
parameters.description: Enables the output.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The output type.

parameters.name: Divisor
parameters.description: The clock division to generate.

parameters.name: Low voltage
parameters.description: The output voltage when the clock is low/inactive.

parameters.name: High voltage
parameters.description: The output voltage when the clock is high/active.

parameters.name: Ratchet mode
parameters.description: The ratchet mode.

parameters.name: Ratchet
parameters.description: The ratchet division for the clock. If mode is “Twos”, selects from 1, 2, 4, 8, 16. If mode is “Twos and threes”, selects from 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16.

parameters.name: Trigger length
parameters.description: The length of the trigger pulse, if the type is “Reset” or “Trigger”.

parameters.name: ES-5 Expander
parameters.description: If set, the ES-5 expander header to use for output instead of the bus specified by the Output parameter. “1” means the ES-5 itself.

input_ports.name: Run/Stop Input
input_ports.description: Input to start and stop the external clock.

input_ports.name: Run/stop input
input_ports.description: The run/stop input for the external clock.

output_ports.name: MIDI Output
output_ports.description: MIDI clock output via Breakout, Select Bus, USB, or Internal.

output_ports.name: ES-5 Output
output_ports.description: If using an ES-5 expander output, sets which of the 8 outputs on the expander to use.
